pre_check_flags.detail: Diff size looks larger than expected: changed_lines=9796, expected≈10, ratio=979.60, files=20, hunks=100.

criteria.name: Existing attachment/embed behavior preserved
criteria.evidence: Targeted suite ran with 194 passed and one executor-identified pre-existing lease-status failure unrelated to media refs. Core tests include existing attachment/embed normalize/render behavior.

criteria.name: normalize_media_ref external support
criteria.evidence: src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py accepts kind values attachment/embed/external and preserves attachment_index shorthand in normalize_media_ref.

criteria.name: Shared deterministic external URL helper used everywhere
criteria.evidence: src/common/external_media.py defines extract_external_urls and most callers use it, but _resolve_media_url_from_metadata re-scans using duplicated _resolve_external_url_by_index in src/features/summarising/topic_editor.py. That duplicate already diverges/bugs out for embed string URLs.

criteria.name: Source payloads expose compact external refs
criteria.evidence: TopicEditor._message_payload and StorageHandler.get_topic_editor_message_context append compact external entries after attachment/embed entries using extract_external_urls.

criteria.name: Explicit attachment/embed/external branches
criteria.evidence: Validation and _resolve_media_url_from_metadata now branch explicitly on attachment, embed, and external rather than treating non-attachment as embed.

criteria.name: Resolver safety controls
criteria.evidence: external_media_resolver enforces safelisted source provenance, long-form fallback policy, yt-dlp metadata-first flow, download size/content-type/Discord compatibility checks, and sanitized URLs.

criteria.name: yt-dlp metadata first and nonblocking failures
criteria.evidence: ExternalMediaResolver.resolve runs _run_yt_dlp before download; failure outcomes return ResolverResult and TopicEditor falls back to URL text.

criteria.name: Stable cache by URL key/content hash
criteria.evidence: Resolver uses make_cache_key(source_url), stores final files with content hash, and DB/storage wrappers plus staged migration add external_media_cache.

criteria.name: Structured send units preserve publish accounting
criteria.evidence: _build_send_units creates text/url/file units; _publish_topic preserves flat_messages, media_indices, media_count, flat_message_count, sent_ids, partial/failed/sent semantics.

criteria.name: Publisher fallback continues after failures
criteria.evidence: _send_one_unit sends fallback_url on resolver/file-send failure and runtime tests cover continuation around failed external refs.

criteria.name: Source media counts do not inflate resolvable_media
criteria.evidence: _summarize_source_media_counts adds external_links separately and tests assert external links are not merged into resolvable_media.

criteria.name: No direct auto-post at five reactions
criteria.evidence: Runtime regression test asserts reaction-qualified media creates watching state and not sent publication status.

criteria.name: Sanitized logs/trace metadata
criteria.evidence: Resolver and publisher traces use sanitise_url_for_logs for URL-bearing logs/traces.

criteria.name: Publish-time fallback URL de-duplication
criteria.evidence: New runtime finding required skipping fallback-link-only/external media units when the URL already appears in associated block text or was already sent. render_topic_publish_units only dedupes media URLs against emitted_media_keys and does not check block_content/block_text, so duplicate Discord unfurls can still occur.

criteria.name: Pure helpers separated from side effects
criteria.evidence: Pure parsing/safety helpers live in src/common/external_media.py; side-effectful yt-dlp/download/filesystem orchestration is in external_media_resolver.

criteria.name: Tests use stubs/fakes
criteria.evidence: Resolver/runtime tests use monkeypatch/fakes for yt-dlp, download, Discord channel sends, and discord.File.

criteria.name: Manual production smoke test
criteria.evidence: Requires deployed Discord/Supabase environment and human visual inspection; listed as deployment follow-up.

issues: _resolve_media_url_from_metadata duplicates external URL lookup instead of relying on the shared helper and crashes for string-valued embed URLs.
Publish-time URL de-duplication for external/fallback links in block text is missing, so duplicate Discord previews can still be sent.

rework_items.issue: External fallback lookup duplicates extract_external_urls logic and crashes on embed URL string fields.
rework_items.expected: A single shared helper should provide stable external index lookup, including returning the original URL for index N, and _resolve_media_url_from_metadata should use it without duplicate scanner drift. String-valued embed URLs should resolve normally.
rework_items.actual: _resolve_media_url_from_metadata calls extract_external_urls only for bounds, then calls _resolve_external_url_by_index, which reimplements scanning and returns an undefined local variable for string embed URL fields. Repro: meta={'embeds':[{'url':'https://x.com/u/status/1'}]} with kind external index 0 raises UnboundLocalError.

rework_items.issue: Publish-time URL de-duplication for external fallback URLs is not implemented.
rework_items.expected: If an external/fallback URL already appears in the associated block text, or has already been sent as a media URL for the topic, do not emit an extra bare URL/file/fallback media unit. This is especially required for long-form fallback-only links such as YouTube/Vimeo/Twitch to avoid duplicate Discord unfurls.
rework_items.actual: render_topic_publish_units appends external media units after the text block whenever _resolve_media_url_from_metadata returns a URL, and only dedupes against prior media URLs. It does not check whether the URL is already present in block_content/block_text.

summary: Needs rework. Most of the external media implementation is present, but two hard requirements are not met: the external fallback URL lookup is not actually a single shared index source and has a real crash for embed string URLs, and the requested publish-time URL de-duplication for fallback/external links is missing.

task_verdicts.reviewer_verdict: Pass. Shared common helper exists with safelist, platform policy, URL normalization, cache keys, content checks, and sanitization.
Pass. Resolver implements metadata-first yt-dlp flow, source provenance, fallback-only long-form policy, cache, size/content-type checks, and structured outcomes.
Pass. DB/storage cache wrappers are present and return None on missing handler/cache miss.
Pass. Staged migration exists with IF NOT EXISTS table/index and expected external_media_cache columns.
Pass. normalize_media_ref accepts external and preserves legacy attachment shorthand.
Pass. Payload/context exposure uses extract_external_urls and appends compact external entries after Discord media entries.
Needs rework. Validation/counting branches are mostly correct, but external fallback URL lookup duplicates the shared extractor and crashes on string-valued embed URLs.
Needs rework. Send-unit model exists, but the required URL de-duplication against block text and previously sent fallback URLs is missing.
Pass with coverage gap. Resolver tests are extensive but missed the embed string URL crash in TopicEditor fallback lookup.
Needs expanded coverage. Core tests cover external content URLs but did not catch string-valued embed URL fallback lookup or block-text de-duplication.
Needs expanded coverage. Runtime tests cover resolver fallback/send failures but not duplicate URL suppression when block text already contains the external URL.
Pass for executed commands except known failure. Targeted suite reports 194 passed and one executor-identified pre-existing lease status failure; broader regression passed per executor evidence.

sense_check_verdicts.verdict: Confirmed for extraction determinism, but downstream fallback lookup still duplicates scanning and can drift.
Confirmed. Unsupported content type returns structured resolver outcome.
Confirmed. Cache miss/no handler returns None.
Confirmed. Migration uses IF NOT EXISTS.
Confirmed. External normalize and attachment shorthand both work.
Confirmed in spirit. External entries are appended after attachment/embed entries, with kind-local indices.
Confirmed. external_links is separate from resolvable_media.
Partially confirmed. Send units and fallback exist, but duplicate fallback suppression is missing.
Confirmed. Runtime test covers fallback continuation.
Partially confirmed. Targeted tests ran with one reported pre-existing failure; however missing tests allowed the two review findings through.
